title: linaria vulgaris
intro: Linaria vulgaris, also known as the common toadflax or butterfly plant, is a flowering plant native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Consuming the plant has been reported to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Linaria vulgaris include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create medicinal teas, salves, and ointments.
